title: Getting started with depictr
text: package ships five reproducibly simulated datasets, chosen exercise different family plots. documented names (e.g. ?lexical_decision) load data(): lexical_decision – counterbalanced, crossed reaction-time/accuracy experiment (participant, item, condition, modality, word frequency). mixed models classification plots. wellbeing_survey – cross-sectional survey (life satisfaction, stress, sleep, income, age, ordered education, region) informative missingness. descriptives, correlations, regression missing data. crop_yield – field trial genuine fertiliser--treatment interaction. regression, scatter-trend interaction plots. clinical_trial – two-arm trial separating survival curves rare adverse-event outcome. survival imbalanced classification. monthly_sales – two seasonal monthly series (indoor/outdoor). time-series plots.

title: Getting started with depictr
text: Begin data. explore_bivariate() chooses suitable plot pair variables – scatter trend , numeric.  Turn next model. fitting , coefficient_plot() draws forest plot estimates.  see model implies, effects_plot() traces predicted response one predictor varies.  residual_diagnostics_plot() gathers usual checks fit one panel.  uncertainty, posterior_plot() summarises posterior simulation draws distribution per parameter. real fixed-effect posterior draws Bayesian fit lexical-decision model, shipped package.
code: explore_bivariate(crop_yield, fertiliser, yield) fit <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertiliser + soil_ph + treatment, data = crop_yield) coefficient_plot(fit, order = "descending", title = "Drivers of crop yield") effects_plot(fit, "fertiliser") residual_diagnostics_plot(fit) draws <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "lexdec_draws.rds", package = "depictr")) posterior_plot(draws[c("conditionunrelated", "modalityauditory",                        "word_frequency")],                labels = c(conditionunrelated = "condition",                           modalityauditory = "modality",                           word_frequency = "word frequency"),                title = "Lexical-decision fixed effects (ms)")

title: Getting started with depictr
text: model functions rest tidy_estimates(), turns model, data frame pre-computed estimates, one standard table. plotting functions also accept table, estimates source (Bayesian posteriors, bootstrap intervals, figures taken paper) can supplied directly.

title: Getting started with depictr
text: theme_depictr(), depictr_palette() scale_colour_depictr() style plots :  qualitative palette based Okabe-Ito set (Okabe & Ito, 2008), stays distinguishable common forms colour-vision deficiency; sequential diverging variants available . Preview :  palette_preview() can also simulate colour-vision deficiency, can check palette deuteranope (red-green) see :  Set look whole script depictr_options() (base size, base family, brand accent colours, custom palette), instead passing arguments every call. Called arguments reports current settings:
code: library(ggplot2) ggplot(crop_yield, aes(fertiliser, yield, colour = treatment)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.7) +   scale_colour_depictr() +   theme_depictr() palette_preview(type = "all") palette_preview(cvd = "deutan") depictr_options() #> $base_size #> [1] 11 #>  #> $base_family #> [1] "" #>  #> $brand #> [1] "#005b96" #>  #> $accent #> [1] "#d55e00" #>  #> $reference #> [1] "grey60" #>  #> $palette #> NULL #>  #> $na_value #> [1] "grey80"

title: Getting started with depictr
text: remaining articles go area turn. vignette("exploring-data") covers distributions, categories, bivariate plots, scatter-plot matrices, correlations, missingness, outliers, summary tables new estimation plots. vignette("model-estimates") flagship: forest plots, model comparison, predicted values, interactions, random effects, optimiser checks frequentist--Bayesian-posterior overlay. vignette("diagnostics--uncertainty") covers residuals, GLM-appropriate binned residuals, classification suite (ROC, PR, gains, lift, calibration, thresholds) imbalanced outcome, power curves. Two articles, vignette("multivariate--survival") vignette("time-series"), cover remaining methods.

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: residual_diagnostics_plot() assembles classic panel; influence_plot() qq_plot() zoom particular checks. fit linear model crop-yield trial.   bubble area Cook’s distance, standard measure observation’s influence fitted coefficients (Cook, 1977). vif_plot() checks multicollinearity among predictors:
code: fit <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertiliser + soil_ph, data = crop_yield) residual_diagnostics_plot(fit, title = "Crop-yield model") influence_plot(fit) vif_plot(fit)

what: Diagnostics for a generalised linear model
title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: binary glm raw residuals take values, plain residual-versus-fitted plot hard read. binned_residual_plot() instead splits data equal-count bins fitted values plots mean residual per bin +/- 2 standard-error band (Gelman & Hill, 2007): points sit inside band, scattered around zero. model rare adverse-event outcome clinical trial (10% base rate) baseline biomarker, age treatment arm.  residual_diagnostics_plot() recognises glm switches GLM-appropriate panels (binned residuals Q-Q plot randomized quantile residuals, standard-normal correct model regardless response distribution):
code: gfit <- glm(adverse_event ~ biomarker + age + arm,             data = clinical_trial, family = binomial) binned_residual_plot(gfit, title = "Binned residuals: adverse-event model") residual_diagnostics_plot(gfit, title = "Adverse-event model")

what: Classification on a genuinely imbalanced outcome
title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: adverse-event outcome imbalanced, exactly precision-recall, gains lift charts earn keep. depictr’s classification plots read binomial glm directly, pair vectors, – compare several models – named list models. ROC curve reports AUC. Passing youden = TRUE marks Youden’s J operating point (threshold maximising sensitivity + specificity - 1):  positive class rare precision-recall curve informative ROC curve, ignores many true negatives. baseline positive prevalence; f1 = TRUE marks maximum-F1 operating point:
code: roc_curve_plot(gfit, youden = TRUE) pr_curve_plot(gfit, f1 = TRUE)

previous_headings: Classification on a genuinely imbalanced outcome
what: Comparing two models in one figure
title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: named list overlays one colour-coded curve per model, per-curve AUC (average precision) legend. full model compared biomarker-model. ROC curve hugs top-left, bottom-right corner always free, legend_inside = TRUE tucks per-model legend reclaims right-hand margin:   ranking targeting tasks, cumulative gains lift charts show many positive cases captured score-ordered population targeted – overlaying models:
code: reduced <- glm(adverse_event ~ biomarker, data = clinical_trial,                family = binomial) models <- list(Full = gfit, `Biomarker only` = reduced) roc_curve_plot(models, youden = TRUE, legend_inside = TRUE) pr_curve_plot(models, f1 = TRUE) gain_plot(models, legend_inside = TRUE) lift_plot(models, legend_inside = TRUE)

previous_headings: Classification on a genuinely imbalanced outcome
title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: threshold_plot() sweeps decision threshold across full range scores plots metric trades , marking Youden maximum-F1 optimal thresholds dashed lines. natural companion ROC PR curves actually choosing cut-.
code: tp <- threshold_plot(gfit, title = "Metrics across the decision threshold") tp attr(tp, "thresholds")   # the Youden and max-F1 thresholds #>    youden        f1  #> 0.1315540 0.2128417

previous_headings: Classification on a genuinely imbalanced outcome
title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: calibration_plot() checks whether predicted probabilities match observed frequencies. bin’s observed rate carries Wilson binomial confidence interval, bins backed observations – common sparse upper tail outcome rare – -interpreted.  confusion-matrix heatmap completes picture. Passing threshold = "youden" reuses operating point ROC curve marks, two agree:
code: calibration_plot(gfit, bins = 6) confusion_matrix_plot(gfit, threshold = "youden", normalise = "row")

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: posterior_plot() summarises draws (posterior, bootstrap, simulation) distribution per parameter. real posterior draws Bayesian fit lexical-decision model, shown point--interval forests.
code: draws <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "lexdec_draws.rds", package = "depictr")) posterior_plot(draws[c("conditionunrelated", "modalityauditory",                        "word_frequency")],                labels = c(conditionunrelated = "condition",                           modalityauditory = "modality",                           word_frequency = "word frequency"),                style = "interval", title = "Posterior estimates (ms)")

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: power_curve_plot() reads simr::powerCurve() object tidy data frame, slow power simulation re-run redraw . package ships real powerCurve object simr analysis lexical-decision design (power condition effect across numbers participants); read straight disk.
code: pc <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "powercurve_lexdec.rds", package = "depictr")) power_curve_plot(pc, x_lab = "Number of participants",                  title = "Power for the condition effect") power_curve_plot(pc_df, x_lab = "Number of participants",                  title = "Power for the condition effect")

title: Diagnostics, classification and uncertainty
text: Combine arrange_plots() save save_plot():
code: arrange_plots(   qq_plot(fit), influence_plot(fit),   ncol = 2, title = "Diagnostics", tag_levels = "A" )

title: Exploring data
text: explore_distribution() numeric variable, explore_categorical() categorical one. unimodal density leaves upper corners empty, legend_inside = TRUE tucks colour legend top-right rather spending right-hand margin . Several plots take argument; default empty corner depends data.  overlay gets crowded several groups, facet = TRUE gives group panel:  ecdf_plot() bin-free alternative: empirical cumulative distribution lets read medians quantiles straight curve makes shift groups obvious (related condition faster throughout).
code: explore_distribution(lexical_decision, RT, group = condition, type = "density",                      legend_inside = TRUE) explore_distribution(wellbeing_survey, life_satisfaction, group = region,                      type = "both", facet = TRUE) ecdf_plot(lexical_decision, RT, group = condition,           reference_quantiles = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75), legend_inside = TRUE) explore_categorical(wellbeing_survey, education, group = region,                     proportion = TRUE, position = "dodge")

title: Exploring data
text: explore_bivariate() selects appropriate plot automatically: scatter plot two numeric variables, box plots numeric variable categorical one, filled bar chart two categorical variables.  focused scatter fitted trend, use scatter_trend(). crop-yield trial real fertiliser--treatment interaction, visible two diverging slopes.
code: explore_bivariate(lexical_decision, condition, RT) scatter_trend(crop_yield, fertiliser, yield, group = treatment)

title: Exploring data
text: explore_pairs() scatter-plot matrix; correlation_heatmap() condenses information single coloured grid.   reorder = TRUE orders variables hierarchical clustering, blocks mutually correlated variables sit together structure easier read:
code: explore_pairs(crop_yield, cols = c("rainfall", "fertiliser", "soil_ph", "yield")) correlation_heatmap(wellbeing_survey) correlation_heatmap(wellbeing_survey, reorder = TRUE)

title: Exploring data
text: raincloud_plot() shows density, box summary raw points together, group_comparison_plot() adds group means confidence intervals raw data. showing estimate alongside uncertainty, latter conveys whether groups differ faithfully bar chart.   compare shape distribution across several groups , ridgeline_plot() stacks one partially overlapping density per group:
code: raincloud_plot(lexical_decision, RT, group = condition) group_comparison_plot(lexical_decision, RT, condition) ridgeline_plot(wellbeing_survey, life_satisfaction, region)

what: Estimation plots: effect size, not just a p-value
title: Exploring data
text: estimation plot puts effect size uncertainty centre comparison. estimation_plot() draws classic Gardner-Altman two-group plot: raw data group means top, mean difference bootstrap confidence interval beneath, aligned difference zero sits reference group’s mean. two groups also annotates standardised effect size (Hedges’ g default).  group_comparison_plot(differences = TRUE) idea reached group-means plot: appends difference panel, turning means comparison full estimation plot. two groups every group compared chosen reference (Cumming plot).
code: set.seed(1) estimation_plot(lexical_decision, RT, condition,                 title = "RT difference: unrelated vs. related priming") set.seed(1) group_comparison_plot(crop_yield, yield, treatment, differences = TRUE,                       title = "Yield difference: enhanced vs. standard")

title: Exploring data
text: dumbbell_plot() compares one value two groups across set categories: two group values per category joined segment, size direction gap clear glance. contrasts younger older respondents’ life satisfaction region.
code: wb <- wellbeing_survey wb$age_group <- ifelse(wb$age < median(wb$age), "younger", "older") dumbbell_plot(wb, region, life_satisfaction, age_group, legend_inside = TRUE)

title: Exploring data
text: wellbeing_survey informative missingness – income missing often higher stress – missingness map worth look modelling.
code: outlier_plot(crop_yield, yield) missingness_map(wellbeing_survey, legend_inside = TRUE)

title: Exploring data
text: summary_table() builds “Table 1”: mean (SD) numeric variables, counts percentages categorical ones, optionally group. first row always reports sample size (N), variable missing values gets Missing, n (%) row – visible wellbeing survey. returns plain data frame, ready knitr::kable().
code: tab <- summary_table(   wellbeing_survey,   vars = c("life_satisfaction", "income", "stress", "education"),   group = "region" ) knitr::kable(tab)

title: Exploring data
text: Every depictr function returns plain ggplot2 object (composite figures return patchwork), can keep adding layers, scales, labels theme tweaks usual +:
code: library(ggplot2)  scatter_trend(crop_yield, fertiliser, yield, group = treatment) +   labs(title = "Yield rises with fertiliser",        subtitle = "More steeply under the enhanced treatment") +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Exploring data
text: levels speak legend title just clutter – drop mapping title NULL. Reversing discrete colour legend time makes read top--bottom order curves stacked:  keep legend title, centring keys often looks tidier ggplot2’s default left alignment:
code: ecdf_plot(lexical_decision, RT, group = condition) +   labs(colour = NULL) +   guides(colour = guide_legend(reverse = TRUE)) correlation_heatmap(wellbeing_survey) +   theme(legend.position = "top") +   guides(fill = guide_colourbar(title.position = "top", title.hjust = 0.5))

what: Moving the legend into the plot
title: Exploring data
text: Several plots take legend_inside = TRUE tuck legend corner usually leave empty (see e.g. ?ecdf_plot). plot , move one theme() call. dodged bar chart, instance, leaves top-right clear right-category short, region legend fits – centre title :
code: explore_categorical(wellbeing_survey, education, group = region,                     proportion = TRUE, position = "dodge") +   theme(legend.position = "inside",         legend.position.inside = c(0.98, 0.98),         legend.justification = c(1, 1),         legend.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5))

title: Exploring data
text: Many functions also expose layout controls directly. explore_distribution(facet = TRUE) ridgeline_plot() separate groups, correlation_heatmap(reorder = TRUE) clusters variables, model-estimate plots take facet standardise keep coefficients different scales legible – see vignette("model-estimates").

title: Visualising model estimates
text: coefficient_plot() reads fitted mixed model directly (tidy_estimates()) draws horizontal point--interval (“forest”) plot. Unrelated primes auditory presentation slow responses, frequent words speed .  Coefficient names tidied effect (variable) name automatically (conditionunrelated becomes condition, word_frequency becomes word frequency); pass labels override . slopes also sit quite different scales, standardise = TRUE rescales coefficient predictor’s standard deviation, putting one comparable axis:  keep raw units instead – including large intercept otherwise squash slopes – facet = TRUE gives term free-scaled panel (layout frequentist-vs-Bayesian comparison uses default).
code: coefficient_plot(   fit, order = "ascending",   labels = c(conditionunrelated = "Unrelated priming",              modalityauditory = "Auditory modality",              word_frequency = "Word frequency (Zipf)"),   title = "Predictors of lexical-decision RT (ms)" ) coefficient_plot(fit, standardise = TRUE, order = "ascending",                  title = "Standardised predictors of RT")

title: Visualising model estimates
text: compare_models() overlays estimates several models can see coefficient moves specification changes. reduced model (dropping modality) compared full model; model_fit_table() summarises fit.  glm models R2 column reports McFadden’s pseudo-R-squared (McFadden, 1974) place ordinary coefficient determination.
code: reduced <- lmerTest::lmer(   RT ~ condition + word_frequency + (1 | participant) + (1 | item),   data = correct ) compare_models(Reduced = reduced, Full = fit, order = "descending") knitr::kable(model_fit_table(Reduced = reduced, Full = fit))

title: Visualising model estimates
text: effects_plot() shows model predicts one predictor varies, holding others typical values. now works mixed model: predictions use fixed effects (re.form = NA) band built fixed-effect design matrix vcov().  interaction_plot() shows relationship changes across second predictor. lexical-decision model additive, genuine interaction switch crop_yield, whose data-generating process contains real fertiliser--treatment effect: fertiliser raises yield far enhanced treatment standard, slopes diverge.
code: effects_plot(fit, "word_frequency",              title = "Predicted RT across word frequency") crop_fit <- lm(yield ~ fertiliser * treatment + rainfall, data = crop_yield) interaction_plot(crop_fit, "fertiliser", "treatment",                  title = "Fertiliser x treatment interaction")

title: Visualising model estimates
text: package’s namesake capability. Given frequentist fit set Bayesian posterior draws, frequentist_bayesian_plot() draws full posterior distribution term (‘ggdist’ half-eye) overlays frequentist point confidence interval position. see entire shape posterior – just point two limits – next frequentist estimate, two sources two leading colourblind-safe palette colours. draws real fixed-effect posterior brms fit model (1000 draws x 4 parameters), shipped package slow MCMC need re-run. Terms matched canonical label, brms parameter names line frequentist ones automatically.  frequentist confidence interval bulk Bayesian posterior land place – reassuring agreement two paradigms – posterior shows density, skew mass either side zero. ggdist unavailable function falls back point--interval forest plot two sources, Bayesian side supplied summary table instead draws (instance Estimate/Q2.5/Q97.5 brms::fixef()) draws familiar two-source forest plot.
code: draws <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "lexdec_draws.rds", package = "depictr"))  frequentist_bayesian_plot(   fit, draws,   intercept = FALSE,   note_frequentist_no_prior = TRUE,   title = "Frequentist estimate over the full Bayesian posterior" )

title: Visualising model estimates
text: posterior_plot() summarises draws (posterior, bootstrap simulation) distribution per parameter. style = "halfeye" shows density slab point--interval; region practical equivalence (ROPE) can shaded parameter annotated probability direction (posterior mass majority side reference line).  probability direction word_frequency conditionunrelated effectively 100%: posterior sits almost entirely one side zero.
code: draws <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "lexdec_draws.rds", package = "depictr")) slopes <- draws[c("conditionunrelated", "modalityauditory", "word_frequency")]  posterior_plot(   slopes, style = "halfeye", rope = c(-5, 5), pd = TRUE,   labels = c(conditionunrelated = "condition",              modalityauditory = "modality",              word_frequency = "word frequency"),   title = "Fixed-effect posteriors (ms), with ROPE and pd" )

title: Visualising model estimates
text: random_effects_plot() draws caterpillar plot conditional modes (“BLUPs”). Reading fitted model directly, shows -item -participant departures average, sorted, uncertainty – usual way spot unusual groups.
code: random_effects_plot(fit, title = "By-group departures (random intercepts)")

title: Visualising model estimates
text: mixed-model fit stable across optimisers. lme4::allFit() refits model every available optimiser; optimizer_fixef_plot() shows fixed effects side side, one panel per term. Tight clusters mean fit settled; scatter signal fragile solution. package ships allFit() summary model, can plot without re-running (slow) refits. plot accepts tidy data frame optimiser--term values, read straight stored summary.  Every optimiser lands estimate term – points coincide within panel – fit rock solid.
code: af <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "allfit_lexdec.rds", package = "depictr")) fx <- af$fixef    # optimisers x fixed effects opt_long <- data.frame(   optimizer = rep(rownames(fx), times = ncol(fx)),   term      = rep(colnames(fx), each = nrow(fx)),   value     = as.vector(fx) )  optimizer_fixef_plot(   opt_long, title = "Fixed effects across optimisers",   labels = c(conditionunrelated = "condition",              modalityauditory = "modality",              word_frequency = "word frequency") )

title: Visualising model estimates
text: model_report() composes several views (coefficients, effect focal predictor, residuals fitted values Q-Q plot, fit-statistics subtitle) single figure rapid review report appendix. works lm/glm models; use crop-yield model.
code: full <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertiliser + soil_ph + treatment,            data = crop_yield) model_report(full, title = "Crop-yield model")

title: Multivariate analysis and survival
text: pca_plot() runs PCA numeric columns data frame draws biplot: observations projected onto two components, variable loadings arrows. scree_plot() shows much variance component explains.   functions also accept ready-made prcomp() object, can analyse plot several views:
code: num <- c("rainfall", "fertiliser", "soil_ph", "yield") pca_plot(crop_yield, cols = num, group = "treatment",          title = "Crop-yield PCA") scree_plot(crop_yield, cols = num) pc <- prcomp(crop_yield[num], scale. = TRUE) pca_plot(pc, components = c(1, 3))

title: Multivariate analysis and survival
text: cluster_plot() runs k-means shows clusters first two principal components (works number variables), convex hulls labelled centroids. dendrogram_plot() draws hierarchical-clustering tree can cut k groups.
code: cluster_plot(crop_yield, cols = num, k = 3, seed = 1,              title = "Crop-yield clusters") region_means <- aggregate(   cbind(stress, sleep_hours, life_satisfaction, age, income) ~ region,   data = wellbeing_survey, FUN = mean ) rownames(region_means) <- region_means$region dendrogram_plot(region_means[-1], k = 2, title = "Regions clustered")

title: Multivariate analysis and survival
text: Choosing k guesswork. k_diagnostic() evaluates cluster-quality criterion across range k suggests value – average silhouette width (default, Rousseeuw, 1987), within-sum--squares elbow, gap statistic (Tibshirani, Walther & Hastie, 2001). See ?k_diagnostic references.  suggested k underlying table attached attributes: silhouette_plot() shows quality actual clustering, one bar per observation grouped cluster, average silhouette width per cluster overall (dashed line). Wide positive bars well-placed observations; negative bars may belong neighbouring cluster.  diagnostics apply wellbeing survey’s numeric profile:
code: kd <- k_diagnostic(crop_yield, k_range = 2:6, cols = num,                    method = "silhouette") kd  # the diagnostic curve, with the suggested k marked attr(kd, "suggested") #> [1] 2 knitr::kable(attr(kd, "k_table"), digits = 3) cl <- kmeans(scale(crop_yield[num]), centers = attr(kd, "suggested"),              nstart = 10)$cluster silhouette_plot(crop_yield, cl, cols = num,                 title = "Silhouette widths by cluster") wb_num <- c("age", "income", "stress", "sleep_hours",             "exercise_days", "life_satisfaction") wkd <- k_diagnostic(wellbeing_survey, k_range = 2:6, cols = wb_num,                     method = "wss") wcl <- kmeans(scale(na.omit(wellbeing_survey[wb_num])),               centers = attr(wkd, "suggested"), nstart = 10)$cluster silhouette_plot(na.omit(wellbeing_survey[wb_num]), wcl,                 title = sprintf("Wellbeing survey, k = %d", attr(wkd, "suggested")))

title: Multivariate analysis and survival
text: survival_plot() draws Kaplan-Meier curves (Kaplan & Meier, 1958) stepwise confidence limits Greenwood’s formula (Greenwood, 1926) censoring marks. estimate computed base R, modelling package needed; can pass follow-times event indicator directly, data frame, survival::survfit object. clinical_trial dataset two arms real survival difference – treatment arm lower hazard – curves separate. Turning three publication annotations gives survminer-style figure: number--risk table beneath curves, dashed guides arm’s median survival, log-rank test difference subtitle. survival curve monotone-decreasing, bottom-left corner always empty – legend_inside = TRUE puts group legend .  log-rank p-value tiny median survival clearly longer treatment arm – event times longer, arm often right-censored 36-month study end, visible denser run censoring marks.
code: survival_plot(   clinical_trial$time, clinical_trial$event, group = clinical_trial$arm,   risk_table = TRUE, median_line = TRUE, logrank = TRUE, legend_inside = TRUE,   x_lab = "Months", title = "Overall survival by arm" )

title: Time series
text: timeseries_plot() accepts ts object, numeric vector, data frame time, value (optionally) group columns. Passing long-form data frame grouping column draws series , colour; moving-average overlay one argument away.  single-series views (decomposition, autocorrelation, seasonal plot forecasting) extract one line monthly ts object frequency 12.
code: timeseries_plot(monthly_sales, time = date, value = sales, group = series,                 rolling = 12, title = "Monthly sales by product line",                 y_lab = "Units") indoor <- subset(monthly_sales, series == "indoor") indoor <- indoor[order(indoor$date), ] indoor_ts <- ts(indoor$sales, start = c(2018, 1), frequency = 12)

title: Time series
text: decompose_plot() separates seasonal series trend, seasonal remainder components. Use method = "stl" (default, loess-based) method = "classical".
code: decompose_plot(indoor_ts, title = "Indoor sales, decomposed")

title: Time series
text: acf_plot() draws autocorrelation (, type = "partial", partial autocorrelation) function, approximate significance bounds. spikes multiples twelve annual seasonality.
code: acf_plot(indoor_ts) acf_plot(indoor_ts, type = "partial")

title: Time series
text: seasonal_plot() draws seasonal-subseries (cycle) plot: one small panel per month, value traced across successive years reference line month’s mean. shows seasonal shape (differences panels) year--year trend within month (slope inside panel) time – something single overlaid line .  style = "season" every year becomes line months shared axis, handy spotting unusual year.
code: seasonal_plot(indoor_ts, title = "Indoor sales: monthly subseries") seasonal_plot(indoor_ts, style = "season",               title = "Indoor sales: one line per year")

title: Time series
text: ts_forecast() lightweight, dependency-free forecaster: decomposes series STL, extrapolates recent trend, carries seasonal pattern forward, returns point forecasts prediction intervals widen horizon. Passing integer horizon straight timeseries_plot() overlays forecast history: point forecast continues line shaded ribbon shows (growing) 90% prediction interval.  fully specified statistical model, fit (example forecast::forecast()) pass resulting time/fit/lwr/upr columns timeseries_plot(forecast = ) data frame.

what: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
text: depictr single, consistent toolkit publication-ready plots span whole analysis workflow, first look data, model estimates predictions, diagnostics, uncertainty reporting. packages address one part work; depictr aims cover end end one theme, one palette one set label conventions. Every plotting function returns ggplot2 object (patchwork composite panels), plot can refined usual + syntax. grew three focused plotting functions (frequentist_bayesian_plot, plot.fixef.allFit powercurvePlot), generalised unified one coherent package.

title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("pablobernabeu/depictr")

title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
text: Explore data: explore_distribution(), ecdf_plot(), explore_categorical(), explore_bivariate(), explore_pairs(), correlation_heatmap(), missingness_map(), outlier_plot(), raincloud_plot(), ridgeline_plot(), group_comparison_plot(), estimation_plot(), dumbbell_plot(), scatter_trend(), summary_table(). Multivariate, clustering & survival: pca_plot(), scree_plot(), cluster_plot(), silhouette_plot(), k_diagnostic(), dendrogram_plot(), survival_plot(). Time series: timeseries_plot(), acf_plot(), decompose_plot(), seasonal_plot(), ts_forecast(). Model estimates & inference: tidy_estimates(), coefficient_plot(), compare_models(), frequentist_bayesian_plot(), effects_plot(), interaction_plot(), random_effects_plot(), optimizer_fixef_plot(), model_fit_table(). Diagnostics & classification: residual_diagnostics_plot(), binned_residual_plot(), influence_plot(), qq_plot(), vif_plot(), roc_curve_plot(), pr_curve_plot(), gain_plot(), lift_plot(), calibration_plot(), threshold_plot(), confusion_matrix_plot(). Uncertainty & power: posterior_plot(), power_curve_plot(). Theming & reporting: theme_depictr(), depictr_palette() / scale_colour_depictr(), palette_preview(), format_terms(), model_report(), arrange_plots(), save_plot(), depictr_options(). Heavier modelling back-ends (lme4, broom, simr) optional (Suggests) used present; core functions, examples, tests vignettes run base lm/glm bundled data alone.

what: How depictr relates to other packages
title: A Unified Toolkit for Visualising Statistical Models and Data
text: depictr aims breadth consistency across workflow, complements specialised packages rather replacing . deeper treatment one area, several remain valuable, among ggstatsplot (statistical details plots), sjPlot easystats family (see, parameters, performance), marginaleffects / ggeffects (predictions), GGally (pairs), factoextra (PCA clustering), survminer (survival), feasts / ggfortify (time series), ggdist / dabestr (distributions estimation) bayesplot / tidybayes (Bayesian). depictr offers consistent attractive default tasks within single package.

title: Binned-residual plot for a generalised linear model — binned_residual_plot
text: fitted glm (especially binary count models), plot raw residuals fitted values hard read residuals take values. Following Gelman Hill (2007), plot instead splits data equal-count bins fitted values plots, bin, mean residual mean fitted value. well-specified model binned residuals scatter around zero, 95% fall within grey \(\pm 2\) standard-error band, computed per bin \(2\,\hat{\sigma}_{\text{bin}}/\sqrt{n_{\text{bin}}}\) residuals bin. Systematic departures (trend, many points outside band) indicate misspecified mean structure.

Use NA omit . point_size, line_size Size points interval lines. facet Whether give term panel free x-axis, laid one per row. removes squish occurs terms live different scales (example large intercept alongside small slopes). Defaults FALSE, preserving shared-axis layout. convenience alias scales = "free". scales Either "fixed" (default, single shared x-axis) "free" (one free-scaled panel per term). facet = TRUE forced "free". standardise Whether standardise coefficients multiplying standard deviation predictor column, putting common scale magnitudes comparable (removing empty band otherwise appears predictors different scales). Requires fitted model (ignored, warning, tidy data frame). Defaults FALSE. title, subtitle, x_lab Plot title, subtitle x-axis label. x_lab defaults "Estimate", "Standardised estimate" standardise.

text: depictr reads small set global options can configure shared look every plot , top script .Rprofile, instead passing arguments function. options honoured theme_depictr() (base size, base family accent used titles), depictr_palette() scale_colour_depictr() family (optional custom qualitative palette), colour accessors depictr_brand(), depictr_accent() depictr_reference().

title: Get or set the depictr look-and-feel options — depictr_options
code: depictr_options(   base_size,   base_family,   brand,   accent,   reference,   palette,   na_value )

title: Get or set the depictr look-and-feel options — depictr_options
text: base_size Base font size points theme_depictr(). base_family Base font family theme_depictr(). brand depictr brand colour (used plot titles lead qualitative colour). Returned depictr_brand(). accent secondary highlight colour. Returned depictr_accent(). reference colour used reference / annotation lines. Returned depictr_reference(). palette optional custom qualitative palette: character vector hex colours used depictr_palette() (type "qualitative") discrete scales place built-Okabe-Ito set. NULL restores built-palette. na_value Colour used NA levels scale_colour_depictr() scale_fill_depictr().

title: Get or set the depictr look-and-feel options — depictr_options
text: named list resolved option values. setting, previous values returned invisibly.

title: Get or set the depictr look-and-feel options — depictr_options
text: Called arguments, depictr_options() returns currently resolved values (option set, otherwise package default). Called named arguments sets matching options(depictr.<name> = ) entries returns previous resolved values invisibly, pattern old <- depictr_options(...); .exit(.call(depictr_options, old)) restores . Pass NULL argument clear option fall back package default.

title: The depictr colour palettes — depictr_palette
text: Colourblind-aware palettes shared every depictr plot. qualitative palette based Okabe-Ito set (Okabe & Ito, 2008), widely recommended categorical palette stays distinguishable common forms colour-vision deficiency, depictr brand blue leading. sequential diverging palettes perceptually ordered single-hue red-blue ramps.

title: The depictr colour palettes — depictr_palette
code: depictr_palette(n = NULL, type = c("qualitative", "sequential", "diverging"))

title: The depictr colour palettes — depictr_palette
text: n Number colours return. NULL (default) full qualitative palette returned. qualitative palette n larger available base colours interpolated; sequential diverging palettes ramps accept n. type Palette type: "qualitative" (categorical groups), "sequential" (ordered low--high) "diverging" (midpoint two directions).

title: The depictr colour palettes — depictr_palette
text: character vector hex colour codes.

title: The depictr colour palettes — depictr_palette
text: qualitative palette can overridden globally options(depictr.palette = ) (see depictr_options()); set, custom palette replaces built-Okabe-Ito set interpolated colours requested provides. sequential diverging ramps unaffected.

title: The depictr colour palettes — depictr_palette
text: Okabe M, Ito K (2008). “Color Universal Design (CUD): Make Figures Presentations Friendly Colorblind People.” https://jfly.uni-koeln.de/color/. Accessed 2026-06-14.

title: The depictr colour palettes — depictr_palette
code: depictr_palette(3) #> [1] "#005b96" "#e69f00" "#009e73" depictr_palette(7, type = "sequential") #> [1] "#E6EFF5" "#98BFDA" "#4A8FC0" "#2475AB" "#005B96" "#034678" "#08315A" scales::show_col(depictr_palette())

title: Dumbbell plot — dumbbell_plot
text: Compares numeric value exactly two groups across set categories: category two group values drawn points joined connecting segment, size direction gap read glance. clearer alternative paired grouped bars /, two-condition two-period comparisons.

title: Dumbbell plot — dumbbell_plot
code: dumbbell_plot(   data,   category,   value,   group,   sort = c("gap", "value", "none"),   point_size = 3,   palette = NULL,   legend_inside = FALSE,   title = NULL,   x_lab = NULL,   y_lab = NULL )

title: Dumbbell plot — dumbbell_plot
text: data data frame. category categorical axis (string unquoted name); one row plot per level. value numeric value compare (string unquoted name). group two-level grouping variable whose levels two ends dumbbell (string unquoted name). sort order categories axis: "gap" (signed difference two groups, default), "value" (second group's value), "none" (data's order). point_size Size end points. palette Length-2 colours two groups; defaults depictr_palette(). legend_inside TRUE, draw two-group legend inside panel – top-right corner, default gap sort (shortest dumbbell top) usually leaves clear – translucent background, instead right-hand margin. Defaults FALSE. title, x_lab, y_lab Plot title axis labels.

title: Dumbbell plot — dumbbell_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Dumbbell plot — dumbbell_plot
text: category several rows per group values averaged. Rows missing category, value group dropped.

title: Dumbbell plot — dumbbell_plot
code: wb <- wellbeing_survey wb$age_group <- ifelse(wb$age < median(wb$age), "younger", "older") dumbbell_plot(wb, region, life_satisfaction, age_group)

what: Empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) plot — ecdf_plot
title: Empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) plot — ecdf_plot
text: Draws empirical CDF numeric variable – proportion observations value – optionally split grouping variable. Unlike histogram needs bin-width choice makes quantiles, medians group shifts easy read directly curve.

title: Empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) plot — ecdf_plot
code: ecdf_plot(   data,   x,   group = NULL,   reference_quantiles = NULL,   palette = NULL,   legend_inside = FALSE,   title = NULL,   x_lab = NULL,   y_lab = "Cumulative proportion" )

title: Empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) plot — ecdf_plot
text: data data frame. x numeric variable (string unquoted name). group Optional grouping variable (string unquoted name) mapped colour, giving one ECDF per group. reference_quantiles Optional numeric vector probabilities [0, 1] mark light horizontal guides (e.g. c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75)); NULL (default) draws none. palette Colours groups; defaults depictr_palette(). legend_inside TRUE (group given), draw legend inside panel – bottom-right corner ECDF leaves empty saturates – translucent background, instead right-hand margin. Defaults FALSE. title, x_lab, y_lab Plot title axis labels.

title: Empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) plot — ecdf_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Empirical cumulative distribution function (ECDF) plot — ecdf_plot
code: ecdf_plot(lexical_decision, RT)  ecdf_plot(lexical_decision, RT, group = condition,           reference_quantiles = c(0.25, 0.5, 0.75))

what: Plot predicted values for one predictor — effects_plot
title: Plot predicted values for one predictor — effects_plot
text: Shows values model predicts one focal predictor varies, holding predictors typical values (mean numeric predictors, frequent level factors). confidence band (numeric predictor) confidence intervals (factor predictor) convey uncertainty.

title: Plot predicted values for one predictor — effects_plot
code: effects_plot(   model,   predictor,   conf_level = 0.95,   n = 100,   rug = TRUE,   colour = depictr_brand(),   title = NULL,   x_lab = NULL,   y_lab = NULL )

title: Plot predicted values for one predictor — effects_plot
text: model fitted model (lm, glm, merMod, ...). predictor Name focal predictor (string). conf_level Confidence level interval. n Number points across range numeric predictor. rug Whether add rug observed predictor values (numeric predictors). colour Colour line/points band. Defaults depictr brand blue. title, x_lab, y_lab Title axis labels.

title: Plot predicted values for one predictor — effects_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot object.

title: Plot predicted values for one predictor — effects_plot
text: Predictions standard errors come stats::predict(); glm predictions formed link scale back-transformed, binomial model shows predicted probabilities. Mixed models fitted lme4::lmer()/lme4::glmer() supported : predictions use fixed effects (re.form = NA) standard errors come fixed-effect design matrix vcov(). Works lm, glm merMod; model classes attempted best-effort basis.

title: Plot predicted values for one predictor — effects_plot
code: fit <- lm(yield ~ rainfall + fertiliser + treatment, data = crop_yield) effects_plot(fit, "fertiliser")  effects_plot(fit, "treatment")   gfit <- glm(accuracy ~ word_frequency + condition,             data = lexical_decision, family = binomial) effects_plot(gfit, "word_frequency")        # predicted probability

title: Gardner-Altman / Cumming estimation plot — estimation_plot
text: estimation plot puts effect size uncertainty centre comparison, rather p-value. upper panel shows group's raw data (jittered) mean confidence interval; lower panel shows pairwise mean difference(s) reference group, bootstrap confidence interval. two panels share aligned outcome axis stacked 'patchwork', difference zero lower panel lines reference group's mean .

title: Gardner-Altman / Cumming estimation plot — estimation_plot
code: estimation_plot(   data,   y,   group,   reference = NULL,   conf_level = 0.95,   n_boot = 5000,   effsize = c("hedges_g", "cohens_d", "none"),   show_points = TRUE,   point_alpha = 0.25,   palette = NULL,   title = NULL,   y_lab = NULL,   heights = c(2, 1.4) )

title: Gardner-Altman / Cumming estimation plot — estimation_plot
text: data data frame. y numeric outcome (string unquoted name). group grouping variable (string unquoted name). reference reference (control) group others compared ; defaults first level group. reference drawn first upper panel sits difference zero lower panel. conf_level Confidence level group intervals (t-based) bootstrap difference intervals. n_boot Number bootstrap resamples difference intervals. effsize Standardised effect size annotated beside difference: "hedges_g" (default, small-sample corrected), "cohens_d", "none" omit . Every contrast reference labelled, standardised effect shown two-group multi-group cases. show_points Whether draw raw data behind group means. point_alpha Transparency raw points. palette Colours groups; defaults depictr_palette(). title, y_lab Title outcome-axis label. heights Relative heights upper (raw data) lower (difference) panels, passed patchwork::plot_layout().

title: Gardner-Altman / Cumming estimation plot — estimation_plot
text: 'patchwork' object (printable like ggplot2::ggplot). computed differences bootstrap intervals attached "differences" attribute (data frame).

title: Gardner-Altman / Cumming estimation plot — estimation_plot
text: exactly two groups classic Gardner-Altman two-group plot (Gardner & Altman, 1986; Ho et al., 2019): single mean difference shown bootstrap interval, annotated standardised effect size (Cohen's d , default, small-sample corrected Hedges' g; Hedges, 1981). two groups becomes Cumming estimation plot (Cumming, 2012): every group compared reference group, difference carrying bootstrap interval. lower-panel interval non-parametric bootstrap mean difference: two groups resampled replacement n_boot times requested percentile interval read resampled differences. makes normality assumption sampling distribution difference. bootstrap uses base R ; set seed beforehand reproducibility. group fewer two observations estimable spread, confidence interval omitted (mean still drawn) difference involving shown point without bootstrap interval; warning issued cases.

title: Plot frequentist and Bayesian estimates together — frequentist_bayesian_plot
text: modernised successor original frequentist_bayesian_plot() gist, built brms::mcmc_plot() show full Bayesian posterior frequentist estimate overlaid. namesake behaviour restored : bayesian carries posterior draws (brms/rstanarm fit, posterior draws object, draws matrix, long/wide draws data frame), full posterior distribution drawn per term ('ggdist' half-eye) frequentist point confidence interval overlaid position. bayesian tidy table posterior summaries (columns term, estimate, conf.low/conf.high, Estimate, l-95% CI, u-95% CI brms::fixef()), function shows familiar two-source forest plot via compare_models(). Terms aligned canonical display label, brms-style b_ prefix reconciled automatically frequentist term names.

title: Plot frequentist and Bayesian estimates together — frequentist_bayesian_plot
code: # Summary path: a tidy "Bayesian" summary as a data frame. freq <- lm(life_satisfaction ~ stress + sleep_hours + exercise_days,            data = wellbeing_survey) bayes <- tidy_estimates(freq) bayes$estimate <- bayes$estimate * 0.95 frequentist_bayesian_plot(freq, bayes,                           title = "Frequentist vs. Bayesian estimates")   # Distribution path: simulated posterior draws (one column per term) drawn as # full posteriors with the frequentist point + CI overlaid. set.seed(1) co <- coef(freq) draws <- as.data.frame(lapply(co, function(m) rnorm(400, m, abs(m) * 0.1 + 0.05))) names(draws) <- names(co) frequentist_bayesian_plot(freq, draws,                           title = "Posterior with frequentist overlay")

title: Simulated lexical-decision experiment — lexical_decision

code: posterior_plot(   draws,   style = c("halfeye", "interval", "gradient", "dots"),   point = c("median", "mean"),   widths = c(0.66, 0.95),   interaction = c("times", "asterisk", "colon", "space"),   labels = NULL,   reference_line = 0,   rope = NULL,   pd = FALSE,   facet = FALSE,   scales = c("fixed", "free"),   colour = depictr_brand(),   fill = depictr_brand(),   rope_fill = depictr_reference(),   rope_alpha = 0.15,   title = NULL,   x_lab = "Value",   caption = NULL )

title: Plot posterior distributions — posterior_plot
text: draws Posterior draws: fitted model (brms/rstanarm), posterior draws object, matrix, long/wide data frame (see Details). style One "halfeye" (density slab + interval, default), "interval" (point two nested intervals, slab), "gradient" (colour-graded interval) "dots" (quantile dotplot). Unknown values missing 'ggdist' fall back "interval". point Central summary: "median" "mean". widths Two interval widths (inner outer), probabilities. outer width used caption displayed interval mass. interaction Passed format_terms() parameter labels. labels Optional named character vector renaming parameters, e.g. c(conditionunrelated = "condition"). Unmatched parameters fall back format_terms(). reference_line Position vertical reference line, NULL/NA omit . universally meaningful reference every parameter, defaults 0 (usual "effect" line differences slopes) set cleared deliberately. rope Optional length-2 numeric c(lo, hi) giving region practical equivalence shade behind distributions. pd TRUE, annotate parameter probability direction relative reference_line – posterior probability parameter lies majority side reference (value [0.5, 1]). Requires finite reference_line. facet Whether give parameter panel free x-axis, laid one per row. keeps small parameters legible large one (typically intercept) otherwise stretch shared axis squish rest. Defaults FALSE. convenience alias scales = "free". scales Either "fixed" (default, single shared x-axis) "free" (one free-scaled panel per parameter). facet = TRUE forced "free". colour, fill Colours point/interval density slab. Default depictr brand blue. rope_fill, rope_alpha Fill colour opacity ROPE band. title, x_lab Plot title value-axis label. caption Plot caption. default (NULL) auto-captions interval mass; pass NA omit caption.

title: Plot posterior distributions — posterior_plot
text: Draws may supplied many shapes: fitted Bayesian model (brms rstanarm), posterior draws object, wide data frame matrix one column draws per parameter, long data frame (parameter column value column). fitted models fixed-effect (population) parameters kept, brms b_ prefix stripped. slab styles use 'ggdist' package. 'ggdist' installed function falls back point--nested-interval display (equivalent style = "interval") emits informative message.

title: Plot posterior distributions — posterior_plot
code: # Wide draws: one column per parameter set.seed(1) draws <- data.frame(   intercept = rnorm(400, 5, 0.3),   slope = rnorm(400, 0.8, 0.15),   `slope:group` = rnorm(400, -0.2, 0.2),   check.names = FALSE ) posterior_plot(draws)   # A region of practical equivalence and a probability-of-direction label posterior_plot(draws, rope = c(-0.1, 0.1), pd = TRUE)   # When one parameter dwarfs the others, give each its own free-scaled panel: # \donttest{ posterior_plot(draws, facet = TRUE)  # }

text: Plots precision recall binary classifier reports average precision (area curve). precision-recall curve informative ROC curve positive class rare, ignores many true negatives. horizontal line marks -skill baseline (positive-class prevalence). title: Normal quantile-quantile plot — qq_plot
text: Two band constructions offered. "pointwise" analytic: uses large-sample standard error \(\)-th order statistic, \(\mathrm{se} = \frac{\hat\sigma}{\phi(z_i)}\sqrt{p_i(1-p_i)/n}\), around fitted reference line. "simulate" builds Monte-Carlo envelope repeatedly drawing normal samples size taking empirical quantiles simulated order statistics, needs large-sample approximation.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plot — survival_plot
text: time numeric vector follow-times, data frame time, status optional group columns, survfit object. status Event indicator time vector. Either 0/1 convention (0 = censored, 1 = event) survival::Surv() 1/2 convention (1 = censored, 2 = event) accepted; logical values also allowed. codings raise error. group Optional grouping variable (vector, column name time data frame). conf_level Confidence level limits (NA omit ). censor_marks Whether mark censoring times +. risk_table Whether add number--risk table beneath curves (composed 'patchwork'). Defaults FALSE. median_line Whether draw dashed guides group's median survival time label . Defaults FALSE. logrank Whether add log-rank test group difference subtitle (two groups ). Defaults FALSE. risk_breaks Optional numeric vector times report number risk. Defaults curve's x-axis breaks. palette Colours groups; defaults depictr_palette(). legend_inside TRUE (several groups), draw group legend inside panel – bottom-left corner monotone-decreasing survival curve always leaves empty – translucent background, instead right-hand margin. Defaults FALSE. title, x_lab, y_lab Title axis labels.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plot — survival_plot
text: ggplot2::ggplot object, - risk_table = TRUE - 'patchwork' object stacking curves risk table.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plot — survival_plot
text: publication-ready figures plot offers three annotations define "survminer-style" Kaplan-Meier display, behind argument default existing behaviour unchanged: Number--risk table (risk_table = TRUE): small panel composed beneath curves 'patchwork', giving number subjects still risk group x-axis breaks. Median-survival guides (median_line = TRUE): dashed reference lines 0.5 survival level group's median survival time, median value labelled. Groups whose curve never reaches 0.5 (median estimable) skipped. Log-rank test (logrank = TRUE): two groups, chi-squared log-rank statistic p-value added subtitle. survival::survdiff() used 'survival' package installed, otherwise equivalent base-R log-rank test computed risk/event tables.

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plot — survival_plot
text: Kaplan EL, Meier P (1958). “Nonparametric Estimation Incomplete Observations.” Journal American Statistical Association, 53(282), 457–481. doi:10.1080/01621459.1958.10501452 . Greenwood M (1926). Natural Duration Cancer, volume 33 Reports Public Health Medical Subjects. Majesty's Stationery Office, London. log-rank test follows Mantel (1966, doi:10.1002/0471667196.ess1530.pub2 ).

title: Kaplan-Meier survival plot — survival_plot
code: set.seed(1) n <- 200 grp <- sample(c("control", "treated"), n, replace = TRUE) time <- rexp(n, rate = ifelse(grp == "treated", 0.05, 0.1)) cens <- runif(n, 0, 30) obs  <- pmin(time, cens) event <- as.integer(time <= cens) survival_plot(obs, event, group = grp, title = "Survival by treatment")   # survminer-style figure: risk table, median guides and a log-rank test data(clinical_trial) survival_plot(clinical_trial$time, clinical_trial$event,               group = clinical_trial$arm, risk_table = TRUE,               median_line = TRUE, logrank = TRUE,               x_lab = "Months", title = "Overall survival")

title: Variance inflation factor plot — vif_plot
text: single-degree--freedom terms ordinary VIF, \(1/(1-R^2)\). terms span several design-matrix columns (multi-level factors, spline/polynomial bases) function reports generalised VIF Fox Monette (1992): \(R\) correlation matrix (centred) predictor columns, \(R_{11}\) block term \(R_{22}\) block remaining columns, $$\mathrm{GVIF} = \frac{\det(R_{11})\,\det(R_{22})}{\det(R)}.$$ every term single degree freedom bars ordinary VIFs, plain "Variance inflation factor" axis reference line threshold. term spans several columns bars switch comparable \(\mathrm{GVIF}^{1/(2\,\mathrm{df})}\) (single-df term equals \(\sqrt{\mathrm{VIF}}\)) reference line moves \(\sqrt{\mathrm{threshold}}\) accordingly. x-axis kept tight data: every bar comfortably threshold line reported caption rather drawn wide empty band.
